notes: The doctrinally load-bearing terms in 2.36 are avācya-vādān (unutterable / indecent words), bahūn (many), tava ahitāḥ (your enemies), nindantaḥ tava sāmarthyam (disparaging your prowess), and the rhetorical climax tato duḥkhataraṁ nu kim (what is more painful than that). The engineering layer preserves all five: 'indecent words' is rendered explicitly; 'enemies who already wished you would fail' preserves tava ahitāḥ as the specific enemy class (not generic critics); 'disparaging your earned prowess' preserves nindantaḥ + sāmarthyam together; the rhetorical climax is rendered as 'no greater pain than this for someone whose identity is the work.' Shankara's reading (sāmarthyam earned in prior battles, here mapped to prior successful migrations) and Ramanuja's reading (the specific kind of slander a hero cannot publicly answer, here mapped to architecture-review-channel rewriting) are both honored without conflation. The disgrace-argument intensification structure (2.34 → 2.35 → 2.36) is honestly named in the source pack's traditional_meaning_consensus. PASS.
